preview_text:
The model favors Bosnia and Herzegovina at 46%, but rates the outcome as genuinely competitive. Qatar's win probability stands at 23%, leaving a substantial gap; however, the model's 31% draw probability signals material uncertainty about which team will prevail in regulation.

The spread across outcomes reflects a match where the favorite is far from dominant. Bosnia and Herzegovina edges ahead in the model's assessment, yet nearly one-third of simulations end level. Qatar's baseline win chance is meaningful enough that a upset remains plausible under the model's framework, though less likely than a Bosnia victory or stalemate.

The model and market diverge notably on Bosnia's prospects. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both price Bosnia considerably higher—around 62–63%—while assigning Qatar only 14–15%. This represents roughly a 15–17 percentage-point gap on Bosnia's win probability between the model and consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate; the discrepancy warrants attention to any reasoning behind the market's added confidence in Bosnia.
